channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: HEBATRON: A Hebrew-Specialized Open-Weight Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
item.description: arXiv:2605.11255v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Hebatron, a Hebrew-specialized open-weight large language model built on the NVIDIA Nemotron-3 sparse Mixture-of-Experts architecture. Training employs a three-phase easy-to-hard curriculum with continuous anti-forgetting anchoring, followe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, release, fine-tuning, large language model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ReAD: Reinforcement-Guided Capability Distillation for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.11290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Capability distillation applies knowledge distillation to selected model capabilities, aiming to compress a large language model (LLM) into a smaller one while preserving the abilities needed for a downstream task. However, most existing methods treat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, framework, large language model, study, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Macro-Action Based Multi-Agent Instruction Following through Value Cancellation
item.description: arXiv:2605.12655v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) in real-world use cases may need to adapt to external natural language instructions that interrupt ongoing behavior and conflict with long-horizon objectives. However, conditioning rewards on instructions intr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, analysis, API, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning Transferable Latent User Preferences for Human-Aligned Decision Making
item.description: arXiv:2605.12682v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as reasoning modules in many applications. While they are efficient in certain tasks, LLMs often struggle to produce human-aligned solutions. Human-aligned decision making requires accounting for both...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, alignment, framework, large language model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On the Size Complexity and Decidability of First-Order Progression
item.description: arXiv:2605.12691v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Progression, the task of updating a knowledge base to reflect action effects, generally requires second-order logic. Identifying first-order special cases, by restricting either the knowledge base or action effects, has long been a central topic in re...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, framework, analysis, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CHAL: Council of Hierarchical Agentic Language
item.description: arXiv:2605.12718v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent debate has emerged as a promising approach for improving LLM reasoning on ground-truth tasks, yet current methodologies face certain structural limitations: debate tends to induce a martingale over belief trajectories, majority voting acco...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, RAG, framework, experiment, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning When to Act: Communication-Efficient Reinforcement Learning via Run-Time Assurance
item.description: arXiv:2605.12561v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safe reinforcement learning (RL) typically asks $\textit{what}$ an agent should do. We ask $\textit{when}$ it needs to act, and show that a single policy can jointly learn control inputs and communication-efficient timing decisions under a pointwise L...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, framework, study, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning to Decide with AI Assistance under Human-Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2605.12646v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: It is widely agreed that when AI models assist decision-makers in high-stakes domains by predicting an outcome of interest, they should communicate the confidence of their predictions. However, empirical evidence suggests that decision-makers often st...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, paper, experiment, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Rollout On-Policy Distillation via Peer Successes and Failures
item.description: arXiv:2605.12652v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are often post-trained with sparse verifier rewards, which indicate whether a sampled trajectory succeeds but provide limited guidance about where reasoning succeeds or fails. On-policy distillation (OPD) offers denser token-leve...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, tool, prompt, analysis, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: State-Centric Decision Process
item.description: arXiv:2605.12755v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language environments such as web browsers, code terminals, and interactive simulations emit raw text rather than states, and provide none of the runtime structure that MDP analysis requires. No explicit state space, no observation-to-state mapping, n...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, analysis, arxiv, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Population Risk Bounds for Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks Trained by DP-SGD with Correlated Noise
item.description: arXiv:2605.12648v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We establish the first population risk bounds for Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) trained by mini-batch SGD with gradient clipping, covering non-private SGD as well as differentially private SGD (DP-SGD) with Gaussian perturbations that interpolate ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: scShapeBench: Discovering geometry from high dimensional scRNAseq data
item.description: arXiv:2605.12662v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: High-dimensional point cloud data arise across many scientific domains, especially single-cell biology. The shapes or topologies of these datasets determine the types of information that can be extracted. For example, clustered data supports cell-type...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, tool, analysis, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
